verdict: High. Court Reporter scores 67 / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

evidence_ledger.source: Felten et al. (2023) — AI Occupational Exposure scores

reasoning_steps.title: LLM capabilities already handle most tasks that a human court reporter would perform
reasoning_steps.body: The LLM's ability to extract citations, review documents, and transcribe audio is already surpassing the capabilities of human court reporters

action_plan.title: Take a course in transcription or court reporting
action_plan.body: Consider taking an online course to learn more about transcription and court reporting, which can help you stay competitive in the job market

follow_up_suggestions.title: Research the impact of AI on the court reporting industry
follow_up_suggestions.body: Look into recent studies or reports that examine the effects of AI on the court reporting profession and consider how it may change your career path

recent_developments.headline: Anthropic Economic Index maps real AI usage across the workforce
recent_developments.impact: Use is concentrated in writing, coding, and analysis — physical and interpersonal work see far less AI involvement.

recent_developments.headline: OECD: about 60% of jobs in advanced economies are exposed to AI
recent_developments.impact: Exposure does not mean replacement — most exposed roles are expected to be transformed rather than eliminated.

recent_developments.headline: Labor market shifts toward AI-complementary skills
recent_developments.impact: Postings increasingly ask for judgment, verification, and direction of AI tools — not just task execution.
